The decision on state bonuses remains with state governments.

Soaring commodity prices may squeeze the profitability of state-run enterprises, which could derail the anticipated ₹75,000 crore dividend. With petroleum sectors particularly at risk, the government is alert to fluctuations in the situation.

items.summary: Non-banking finance companies anticipate strong growth in the upcoming quarter. However, rising funding costs may limit profit margins. Analysts are closely monitoring asset quality for potential stress in the coming months. The impact of global events on consumer demand and specific sectors is a key concern. NBFCs are preparing for these challenges.

items.title: Diesel export tax hike may align prices of state OMCs, private cos
items.summary: A significant increase in diesel export tax is set to align prices for state-run oil companies and private fuel sellers. This move could end a prolonged dispute over domestic fuel supply agreements. State-run firms typically buy diesel from private refiners and some state-run ones.

items.title: NCLAT upholds project-specific insolvency action for realty firms
items.summary: Homebuyers' insolvency cases against real estate firms will now be limited to the specific project facing default. The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal has ruled that extending these proceedings to other projects of the company is not beneficial for homebuyers or other stakeholders. This decision ensures that unrelated projects are protected.

items.title: Telecom infrastructure, gear makers face disruptions
items.summary: Telecom operations in India are facing significant challenges. The ongoing conflict in West Asia is disrupting tower connectivity and increasing costs for imported equipment. Restrictions on diesel sales are hindering essential refuelling for telecom towers. Power outages are also a concern, increasing reliance on diesel generators. Supply chains for tower manufacturing are affected, potentially delaying network expansion.

items.title: Amid West Asia crisis: Santa Marta meet aims to drive fossil fuel shift
items.summary: The Santa Marta conference has been described as a meeting of the "coalition of doers", comprising countries and stakeholders committed to shifting away from fossil fuels. It will bring together members of existing alliances such as the Fossil Fuel Non-Proliferation Treaty, the Beyond Oil and Gas Alliance, and the Powering Past Coal Alliance.

items.title: Broadcast ecosystem may be headed for regulatory reset
items.summary: India's broadcast sector is set for a major regulatory overhaul. TRAI is planning a broad review of pricing, distribution, and platform rules. This comes after the New Tariff Order faced criticism for not ensuring affordability and transparency. The move aims to address structural concerns and adapt to changing consumer habits and competition from streaming services.

items.title: Social media is abuzz with weight-loss drug retatrutide and longevity goals. What is the drug all about?
items.summary: A new weight-loss drug, retatrutide, is generating excitement.
